# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

class EmailMailer < ActionMailer::Base   # Inherits from Active Mail if not in development mode already for dev/prod environments by default due to inclusion sendgrid-ruby gems as gem 'sendgrid_mail' (unless specified otherwise) unless Rails.env === :development then include SendGridMail 
    def hello(user, msg = "Good morning") # Declare the action that will be triggered inside your mailer class  
      @msg  = "#{msg} from user: #{user}"         
            subject 'Test email'      
             to '"User Name" <test@example.com>',  :from => 'sender name  <smtp_username>  ',cc:'anotheremailaddress1@gmail.com,bcc:"receiver2@domainname.co.uk"'      # Change the values below by replacing with your information  
    end     def world(user)         subject: "Good Afternoon"          to:#{"Another User <#{user}>"}        body "#{'Dear ' + user+ ', thanks for ordering.' }             content_type:'text/html'  # Change the values below by replacing with your information  
    end     def goodbye(to, subject: "Good Bye", message:"Cheers from our side")          to:#{user.email}           body "#{'Dear ' + user+ ', '.concat (message)}             content_type:'text/html'  # Change the values below by replacing with your information  
    end     private :goodbye# This will not work in a standard rails app, it has to be at email level for mailer class. It is used internally only and can’t really send emails outside this context unless you have Sendgrid set up on top of which all these methods would simply call the above defined actions    
    end  # This closing bracket makes sure that everything within inside it closes properly to allow Ruby engine for any subsequent content in file. It is used as a mechanism by rubygems/gem's prepend or append method, not an explicit markup like comments on this line and above  
end
